metadata.author: Check Point - support@checkpoint.com
metadata.comments: Solution template for Check Point Cyberint Alerts

metadata.description: Not used, but needed to pass arm-ttk test `Location-Should-Not-Be-Hardcoded`.  We instead use the `workspace-location` which is derived from the LA workspace
[concat('Region to deploy solution resources -- separate from location selection',parameters('location'))]
Workspace name for Log Analytics where Microsoft Sentinel is setup

workbook1-name.defaultValue: Check Point Exposure Management - Alert Overview

metadata.description: Name for the workbook
resource group name where Microsoft Sentinel is setup
subscription id where Microsoft Sentinel is setup

properties.description: CPEMAlertIngestionAnomaly_AnalyticalRules Analytics Rule with template version 3.1.2

Detects when no Check Point Exposure Management alerts have been ingested into the argsentdc_CL table
for an extended period. This may indicate a failure in the CCP data connector or the
Importer playbook, a misconfigured API token, or network connectivity issues.
properties.displayName: Check Point Exposure Management - Alert Ingestion Anomaly

alertDetailsOverride.alertDisplayNameFormat: Check Point Exposure Management - Ingestion gap detected ({{GapMinutes}} minutes)

properties.description: Check Point Cyberint Alerts Analytics Rule 1

properties.displayName: Check Point Exposure Management - Alert Ingestion Anomaly

metadata.description: Gain insights into Check Point Exposure Management alerts ingested into Microsoft Sentinel. Monitor alert volumes, severity distribution, status changes, and sync health between Microsoft Sentinel and Check Point Exposure Management.

properties.serializedData:
{"version":"Notebook/1.0","items":[{"type":1,"content":{"json":"## Check Point Exposure Management - Alert Overview & Sync Health

This workbook provides visibility into Check Point Exposure Management alerts ingested via the CCP data connector and Importer playbook. Use it to monitor alert volume, status distribution, and ingestion health."},"name":"header"},{"type":9,"content":{"version":"KqlParameterItem/1.0","parameters":[{"id":"a2b3c4d5-e6f7-4a8b-9c0d-1e2f3a4b5c6d","version":"KqlParameterItem/1.0","name":"TimeRange","type":4,"isRequired":true,"typeSettings":{"selectableValues":[{"durationMs":3600000},{"durationMs":14400000},{"durationMs":43200000},{"durationMs":86400000},{"durationMs":259200000},{"durationMs":604800000},{"durationMs":2592000000}],"allowCustom":true},"value":{"durationMs":604800000}}],"style":"pills","queryType":0,"resourceType":"microsoft.operationalinsights/workspaces"},"name":"parameters"},{"type":11,"content":{"version":"LinkItem/1.0","style":"tabs","links":[{"id":"tab-overview","cellValue":"selectedTab","linkTarget":"parameter","linkLabel":"Overview","subTarget":"Overview","style":"link"},{"id":"tab-sync-health","cellValue":"selectedTab","linkTarget":"parameter","linkLabel":"Sync Health","subTarget":"SyncHealth","style":"link"},{"id":"tab-alert-details","cellValue":"selectedTab","linkTarget":"parameter","linkLabel":"Alert Details","subTarget":"AlertDetails","style":"link"}]},"name":"tabs"},{"type":12,"content":{"version":"NotebookGroup/1.0","groupType":"editable","items":[{"type":3,"content":{"version":"KqlItem/1.0","query":"argsentdc_CL
| where TimeGenerated {TimeRange}
| summarize
    TotalAlerts = dcount(ref_id),
    OpenAlerts = dcountif(ref_id, status == "open"),
    ClosedAlerts = dcountif(ref_id, status == "closed"),
    LatestAlert = max(TimeGenerated)
| project
    ['Total Alerts'] = TotalAlerts,
    ['Open'] = OpenAlerts,
    ['Closed'] = ClosedAlerts,
    ['Latest Ingestion'] = LatestAlert","size":4,"timeContext":{"durationMs":0},"timeContextFromParameter":"TimeRange","queryType":0,"resourceType":"microsoft.operationalinsights/workspaces","visualization":"tiles","tileSettings":{"showBorder":true,"titleContent":{"columnMatch":"Total Alerts","formatter":12,"formatOptions":{"palette":"auto"}}}},"name":"summary-tiles"},{"type":3,"content":{"version":"KqlItem/1.0","query":"argsentdc_CL
| where TimeGenerated {TimeRange}
| summarize arg_max(TimeGenerated, status) by ref_id
| summarize Count = count() by status
| order by Count desc","size":2,"title":"Alert Status Distribution","timeContext":{"durationMs":0},"timeContextFromParameter":"TimeRange","queryType":0,"resourceType":"microsoft.operationalinsights/workspaces","visualization":"piechart"},"customWidth":"50","name":"status-distribution"},{"type":3,"content":{"version":"KqlItem/1.0","query":"argsentdc_CL
| where TimeGenerated {TimeRange}
| summarize arg_max(TimeGenerated, severity) by ref_id
| summarize Count = count() by severity
| order by Count desc","size":2,"title":"Alert Severity Distribution","timeContext":{"durationMs":0},"timeContextFromParameter":"TimeRange","queryType":0,"resourceType":"microsoft.operationalinsights/workspaces","visualization":"piechart"},"customWidth":"50","name":"severity-distribution"},{"type":3,"content":{"version":"KqlItem/1.0","query":"argsentdc_CL
| where TimeGenerated {TimeRange}
| summarize AlertCount = dcount(ref_id) by bin(TimeGenerated, 1h)
| order by TimeGenerated asc","size":0,"title":"Alert Ingestion Over Time","timeContext":{"durationMs":0},"timeContextFromParameter":"TimeRange","queryType":0,"resourceType":"microsoft.operationalinsights/workspaces","visualization":"timechart","chartSettings":{"seriesLabelSettings":[{"seriesName":"AlertCount","label":"Distinct Alerts"}]}},"name":"ingestion-timeline"},{"type":3,"content":{"version":"KqlItem/1.0","query":"argsentdc_CL
| where TimeGenerated {TimeRange}
| summarize arg_max(TimeGenerated, category) by ref_id
| summarize Count = count() by category
| order by Count desc
| take 10","size":2,"title":"Top Alert Categories","timeContext":{"durationMs":0},"timeContextFromParameter":"TimeRange","queryType":0,"resourceType":"microsoft.operationalinsights/workspaces","visualization":"barchart"},"name":"top-categories"}]},"conditionalVisibility":{"parameterName":"selectedTab","comparison":"isEqualTo","value":"Overview"},"name":"overview-group"},{"type":12,"content":{"version":"NotebookGroup/1.0","groupType":"editable","items":[{"type":3,"content":{"version":"KqlItem/1.0","query":"argsentdc_CL
| where TimeGenerated {TimeRange}
| summarize RecordCount = count() by bin(TimeGenerated, 10m)
| order by TimeGenerated asc","size":0,"title":"Ingestion Volume (records per 10-min window)","timeContext":{"durationMs":0},"timeContextFromParameter":"TimeRange","queryType":0,"resourceType":"microsoft.operationalinsights/workspaces","visualization":"timechart","chartSettings":{"seriesLabelSettings":[{"seriesName":"RecordCount","label":"Records Ingested"}]}},"name":"ingestion-volume"},{"type":3,"content":{"version":"KqlItem/1.0","query":"argsentdc_CL
| where TimeGenerated > ago(24h)
| summarize RecordCount = count() by bin(TimeGenerated, 10m)
| order by TimeGenerated asc
| extend PreviousTime = prev(TimeGenerated)
| where isnotnull(PreviousTime)
| extend GapMinutes = datetime_diff('minute', TimeGenerated, PreviousTime)
| where GapMinutes > 15
| project GapStart = PreviousTime, GapEnd = TimeGenerated, GapMinutes
| order by GapMinutes desc","size":0,"title":"Ingestion Gaps (>15 minutes, last 24h)","timeContext":{"durationMs":86400000},"queryType":0,"resourceType":"microsoft.operationalinsights/workspaces","visualization":"table","gridSettings":{"formatters":[{"columnMatch":"GapMinutes","formatter":8,"formatOptions":{"palette":"redGreen"}}]}},"name":"ingestion-gaps"},{"type":3,"content":{"version":"KqlItem/1.0","query":"argsentdc_CL
| where TimeGenerated {TimeRange}
| where isnotempty(modification_date)
| extend IngestionDelay = datetime_diff('minute', TimeGenerated, modification_date)
| where IngestionDelay >= 0
| summarize
    AvgDelayMinutes = avg(IngestionDelay),
    P50DelayMinutes = percentile(IngestionDelay, 50),
    P95DelayMinutes = percentile(IngestionDelay, 95),
    MaxDelayMinutes = max(IngestionDelay)
| project
    ['Avg Delay (min)'] = round(AvgDelayMinutes, 1),
    ['P50 Delay (min)'] = round(P50DelayMinutes, 1),
    ['P95 Delay (min)'] = round(P95DelayMinutes, 1),
    ['Max Delay (min)'] = round(MaxDelayMinutes, 1)","size":4,"title":"Ingestion Latency (modification_date → TimeGenerated)","timeContext":{"durationMs":0},"timeContextFromParameter":"TimeRange","queryType":0,"resourceType":"microsoft.operationalinsights/workspaces","visualization":"tiles","tileSettings":{"showBorder":true}},"name":"ingestion-latency"},{"type":3,"content":{"version":"KqlItem/1.0","query":"argsentdc_CL
| where TimeGenerated {TimeRange}
| summarize
    NewAlerts = countif(created_date >= ago(1d)),
    ModifiedAlerts = countif(modification_date >= ago(1d) and modification_date != created_date),
    TotalRecords = count()
    by bin(TimeGenerated, 1h)
| order by TimeGenerated asc","size":0,"title":"New vs Modified Alert Records Over Time","timeContext":{"durationMs":0},"timeContextFromParameter":"TimeRange","queryType":0,"resourceType":"microsoft.operationalinsights/workspaces","visualization":"timechart"},"name":"new-vs-modified"}]},"conditionalVisibility":{"parameterName":"selectedTab","comparison":"isEqualTo","value":"SyncHealth"},"name":"sync-health-group"},{"type":12,"content":{"version":"NotebookGroup/1.0","groupType":"editable","items":[{"type":3,"content":{"version":"KqlItem/1.0","query":"argsentdc_CL
| where TimeGenerated {TimeRange}
| summarize arg_max(TimeGenerated, *) by ref_id
| project
    TimeGenerated,
    ref_id,
    event_title,
    status,
    severity,
    category,
    source,
    confidence,
    created_date,
    modification_date,
    threat_actor
| order by TimeGenerated desc
| take 100","size":0,"title":"Latest Alerts (deduplicated by ref_id)","timeContext":{"durationMs":0},"timeContextFromParameter":"TimeRange","queryType":0,"resourceType":"microsoft.operationalinsights/workspaces","visualization":"table","gridSettings":{"formatters":[{"columnMatch":"severity","formatter":18,"formatOptions":{"thresholdsOptions":"colors","thresholdsGrid":[{"operator":"==","thresholdValue":"very_high","representation":"redBright","text":"{0}"},{"operator":"==","thresholdValue":"high","representation":"red","text":"{0}"},{"operator":"==","thresholdValue":"medium","representation":"orange","text":"{0}"},{"operator":"==","thresholdValue":"low","representation":"green","text":"{0}"},{"operator":"Default","representation":"gray","text":"{0}"}]}},{"columnMatch":"status","formatter":18,"formatOptions":{"thresholdsOptions":"colors","thresholdsGrid":[{"operator":"==","thresholdValue":"open","representation":"blue","text":"{0}"},{"operator":"==","thresholdValue":"closed","representation":"gray","text":"{0}"},{"operator":"Default","representation":"yellow","text":"{0}"}]}}]}},"name":"alerts-table"},{"type":3,"content":{"version":"KqlItem/1.0","query":"argsentdc_CL
| where TimeGenerated {TimeRange}
| summarize arg_max(TimeGenerated, *) by ref_id
| where status == "closed" and isnotempty(closure_reason)
| summarize Count = count() by tostring(closure_reason)
| order by Count desc","size":2,"title":"Closure Reason Breakdown","timeContext":{"durationMs":0},"timeContextFromParameter":"TimeRange","queryType":0,"resourceType":"microsoft.operationalinsights/workspaces","visualization":"piechart"},"customWidth":"50","name":"closure-reasons"},{"type":3,"content":{"version":"KqlItem/1.0","query":"argsentdc_CL
| where TimeGenerated {TimeRange}
| summarize arg_max(TimeGenerated, *) by ref_id
| where isnotempty(threat_actor)
| summarize AlertCount = dcount(ref_id) by threat_actor
| order by AlertCount desc
| take 10","size":2,"title":"Top Threat Actors","timeContext":{"durationMs":0},"timeContextFromParameter":"TimeRange","queryType":0,"resourceType":"microsoft.operationalinsights/workspaces","visualization":"barchart"},"customWidth":"50","name":"threat-actors"}]},"conditionalVisibility":{"parameterName":"selectedTab","comparison":"isEqualTo","value":"AlertDetails"},"name":"alert-details-group"}],"$schema":"https://github.com/Microsoft/Application-Insights-Workbooks/blob/master/schema/workbook.json"}

properties.description: @{workbookKey=CPEMAlertOverviewWorkbook; logoFileName=checkpoint.svg; description=Gain insights into Check Point Exposure Management alerts ingested into Microsoft Sentinel. Monitor alert volumes, severity distribution, status changes, and sync health between Microsoft Sentinel and Check Point Exposure Management.; dataTypesDependencies=System.Object[]; dataConnectorsDependencies=System.Object[]; previewImagesFileNames=System.Object[]; version=1.0.0; title=Check Point Exposure Management - Alert Overview; templateRelativePath=CPEMAlertOverview.json; subtitle=; provider=Check Point}.description

        "other", "other", "other",
        "user_defined_saved_query_hit", "other", "user_defined_saved_query"
    ];
argsentdc_CL
| summarize arg_max(TimeGenerated, *) by ref_id
| lookup kind=leftouter AlertMetadata on event_type
| extend
    alert_category = coalesce(alert_category, category, "unknown"),
    alert_subcategory = coalesce(alert_subcategory, "unknown")

argsentdc_CL
| summarize arg_max(TimeGenerated, *) by ref_id
| lookup kind=leftouter AlertMetadata on event_type
| extend
    alert_category = coalesce(alert_category, category, "unknown"),
    alert_subcategory = coalesce(alert_subcategory, "unknown")

metadata.description: Name of the Logic App / Playbook
Microsoft Sentinel workspace name for KQL queries and incident creation
How often to query for new alerts (minutes)
Create Microsoft Sentinel incidents (possible values true/false)

properties.description: Data Collection Endpoint used by the CPEM Importer playbook to write SecurityAlert rows via the Log Ingestion API.
Routes ingested CPEM alerts into the built-in SecurityAlert table so a bridge analytic rule can promote them to Microsoft Sentinel incidents.

Bridges SecurityAlert rows ingested by the CPEM Importer into Microsoft Sentinel incidents. Surfaces ref_id as a Custom Detail so the CPEM Exporter playbook can read it via properties.alerts[*].properties.additionalData['Custom Details'].
properties.query:
SecurityAlert
| where ProviderName == 'Check Point Exposure Management'
| extend Parsed = parse_json(ExtendedProperties)
| extend RefId = tostring(Parsed.ref_id)
| extend EventType = tostring(Parsed.event_type)
| extend Category = tostring(Parsed.category)
| extend OriginalSeverity = tostring(Parsed.severity)
| extend Confidence = tostring(Parsed.confidence)
| extend Recommendation = tostring(Parsed.recommendation)
| project TimeGenerated, SystemAlertId, DisplayName, AlertSeverity, Description, RefId, EventType, Category, OriginalSeverity, Confidence, Recommendation

inputs.body:
argsentdc_CL
| where TimeGenerated > ago(@{parameters('Polling_Interval_Minutes')}m)
| summarize arg_max(TimeGenerated, *) by tostring(ref_id)
| where status == "open"
| extend sentinel_severity = case(
    severity in ("very_high", "critical"), "High",
    severity == "high", "High",
    severity == "medium", "Medium",
    severity == "low", "Low",
    "Informational"
  )
| project ref_id, event_title, description, severity, sentinel_severity, status,
          confidence, event_type, category, threat_actor,
          recommendation, created_date, modification_date,
          related_assets, impacts, tags, iocs, TimeGenerated

metadata.title: Check Point EM - Importer (Alerts → SecurityAlert → Microsoft Sentinel Incidents)
metadata.description: Queries the argsentdc_CL custom table (populated by the CCP data connector) for recent alerts and writes each one to the built-in SecurityAlert table via the Log Ingestion API. A bundled scheduled analytic rule then promotes those SecurityAlert rows into Microsoft Sentinel incidents with ref_id surfaced as a Custom Detail so the CPEM Exporter playbook can read it.
prerequisites: The CCP data connector must be deployed and ingesting alerts into the argsentdc_CL table.
After deployment, open the playbook and authorize the Azure Monitor Logs and Microsoft Sentinel connections.
postDeployment: Open the playbook in the Logic App designer, authorize both API connections (Azure Monitor Logs and Microsoft Sentinel), and press Save.

The template auto-deploys a Data Collection Endpoint, a SecurityAlert-targeted DCR, a Monitoring Metrics Publisher role assignment for the Logic App Managed Identity, and a Scheduled analytic rule named '<PlaybookName> - Promote CPEM SecurityAlerts to incidents' that converts SecurityAlert rows into Microsoft Sentinel incidents.

releaseNotes.title: Check Point EM - Importer
notes: Initial version - direct API polling with DCR ingestion

releaseNotes.title: Check Point EM - Importer
notes: Rewritten to query argsentdc_CL table and create Microsoft Sentinel incidents (RecordedFuture AlertImporter pattern)

releaseNotes.title: Check Point EM - Importer
notes: Replaced direct incident creation with Log Ingestion API writes into the built-in SecurityAlert table.

Adds a Data Collection Endpoint, a SecurityAlert-targeted DCR, a Monitoring Metrics Publisher role assignment for the Logic App MI, and a Scheduled bridge analytic rule that promotes ingested SecurityAlerts to incidents.
Outcome: incidents now carry the alert object the CPEM Exporter expects, with ref_id available at properties.alerts[*].properties.additionalData['Custom Details'].

metadata.description: Name of the Logic App / Playbook
Base URL of the Check Point Exposure Management API (e.g. https://app.cyberint.io)
API token for Check Point Exposure Management

metadata.title: Check Point Exposure Management - Exporter (Microsoft Sentinel → Argos)
metadata.description: When a Microsoft Sentinel incident status changes, this playbook pushes the update to the corresponding alert(s). Includes tag-based loop prevention to avoid circular sync with Importer.
prerequisites: 1. A valid Check Point Exposure Management API token and base URL.
2. The Logic App Managed Identity must be granted Microsoft Sentinel Responder role on the resource group.
postDeployment: 1. Grant the Logic App Managed Identity the **Microsoft Sentinel Responder** role.
2. Configure an automation rule in Microsoft Sentinel to trigger this playbook on incident status changes.

metadata.description: Name of the Logic App / Playbook
Base URL of the Check Point Exposure Management API (e.g. https://app.cyberint.io)
API token for Check Point Exposure Management

body.message:
CPEM Manual Status Update:
Microsoft Sentinel Status: @{triggerBody()?['object']?['properties']?['status']}
Argos Status: @{outputs('Set_ArgosStatus')}
Closure Reason: @{outputs('Set_ArgosClosureReason')}

Results:
@{variables('SyncResults')}

metadata.title: Check Point Exposure Management - Manual Status Update (Microsoft Sentinel → Argos)
metadata.description: On-demand playbook that reads the current Microsoft Sentinel incident status and pushes it to the corresponding alert(s). Triggered manually from the incident actions menu.
prerequisites: 1. A valid Check Point Exposure Management API token and base URL.
2. The Logic App Managed Identity must be granted Microsoft Sentinel Responder role on the resource group.
postDeployment: 1. Grant the Logic App Managed Identity the **Microsoft Sentinel Responder** role.
2. Analysts can run this playbook from the Microsoft Sentinel incident Actions menu.

releaseNotes.title: Check Point Exposure Management - Manual Status Update

metadata.title: Check Point Exposure Management - Phishing Takedown
metadata.description: When a new Microsoft Sentinel incident is created for a phishing website alert, this playbook extracts the phishing URL, evaluates confidence and severity thresholds, submits a takedown request to Check Point Exposure Management, and tracks the takedown status back to the incident.
prerequisites: 1. A valid Check Point Exposure Management API token and base URL.
2. The Logic App Managed Identity must be granted Microsoft Sentinel Responder role on the resource group.
postDeployment: 1. Grant the Logic App Managed Identity the **Microsoft Sentinel Responder** role.
2. Configure an automation rule in Microsoft Sentinel to trigger this playbook on phishing website incidents.

properties.displayName: Check Point Cyberint Alerts Connector (via Codeless Connector Platform)

connectorUiConfig.title: Check Point Cyberint Alerts Connector (via Codeless Connector Platform)
connectorUiConfig.descriptionMarkdown: Cyberint, a Check Point company, provides a Microsoft Sentinel integration to streamline critical Alerts and bring enriched threat intelligence from the Infinity External Risk Management solution into Microsoft Sentinel. This simplifies the process of tracking the status of tickets with automatic sync updates across systems. Using this new integration for Microsoft Sentinel, existing Cyberint and Microsoft Sentinel customers can easily pull logs based on Cyberint's findings into Microsoft Sentinel platform.

resourceProvider.permissionsDisplayText: Read and Write permissions are required.

customs.name: Check Point Cyberint API Key, Argos URL, Customer Name
customs.description: The connector API key, Argos URL, and Customer Name are required. Include CSV Attachments as JSON is optional.

instructionSteps.title: Connect Checkpoint Cyberint Alerts to Microsoft Sentinel
instructionSteps.description:
To enable the connector provide the required information below and click on Connect.

**Argos URL** — Cyberint API URL for your tenant (e.g. `https://your_tenant.cyberint.io`)

**API Token** — Cyberint API access token

**Customer Name** — Company (client) name associated with your Cyberint instance

**Severity** — Comma-separated list of severities to fetch (low, medium, high, very_high). If empty, all severities are fetched.

**Environments** — Comma-separated list of environments to fetch (e.g. Production,Staging). If empty, all environments are fetched.

**Polling Interval** — How often to poll for new alerts, in minutes (default: 5)

**Include CSV Attachments as JSON** — Whether to include CSV attachments as JSON content in alerts (default: false)
>

parameters.label: Customer Name
parameters.placeholder: Company (client) name associated with your Cyberint instance
parameters.name: customername

dataFlows.transformKql:
source
| extend TimeGenerated = update_date
| extend event_type = type
| extend event_title = ['title']
| project-away update_date, type, ['title']

request.queryParametersTemplate: [[concat('{ ''filters'': { ''created_date'': { ''from'': ''{_QueryWindowStartTime}'', ''to'': ''{_QueryWindowEndTime}'' }', if(not(empty(parameters('severity'))), concat(', ''severity'': [''', replace(parameters('severity'), ',', ''','''), ''']'), ''), if(not(empty(parameters('environments'))), concat(', ''environments'': [''', replace(parameters('environments'), ',', ''','''), ''']'), ''), ' }, ''include_csv_attachments_as_json_content'': ', parameters('includecsvattachments'), ' }')]]

properties.descriptionHtml:
Note: Please refer to the following before installing the solution:

• Review the solution Release Notes

• There may be known issues pertaining to this Solution, please refer to them before installing.

Check Point provides Microsoft Sentinel integration to streamline critical Alerts and bring enriched threat intelligence from the Infinity External Risk Management solution into Microsoft Sentinel. This simplifies the process of tracking the status of tickets with automatic sync updates across systems. Using this new integration for Microsoft Sentinel, existing Check Point Exposure Management and Microsoft Sentinel customers can easily pull logs based on Check Point Exposure Management findings into Microsoft Sentinel platform.

NOTE: Microsoft recommends installation of Check Point Exposure Management Alerts Connector (via Codeless Connector Platform). This connector is build on the Codeless Connector Platform (CCP), which uses the Log Ingestion API, which replaces ingestion via the deprecated HTTP Data Collector API. CCP-based data connectors also support Data Collection Rules (DCRs) offering transformations and enrichment.

Data Connectors: 1, Parsers: 1, Workbooks: 1, Analytic Rules: 1, Playbooks: 8

Learn more about Microsoft Sentinel | Learn more about Solutions
